118	/** Options for a plugin entry that registers providers, tools, commands, or services. */
119	type DefinePluginEntryOptions = {
120	  id: string;
121	  name: string;
122	  description: string;
123	  kind?: OpenClawPluginDefinition["kind"];
124	  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
125	  register: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
126	};
127	
128	/** Normalized object shape that OpenClaw loads from a plugin entry module. */
129	type DefinedPluginEntry = {
130	  id: string;
131	  name: string;
132	  description: string;
133	  configSchema: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema;
134	  register: NonNullable<OpenClawPluginDefinition["register"]>;
135	} & Pick<OpenClawPluginDefinition, "kind">;
136	
137	/** Resolve either a concrete config schema or a lazy schema factory. */
138	function resolvePluginConfigSchema(
139	  configSchema: DefinePluginEntryOptions["configSchema"] = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
140	): OpenClawPluginConfigSchema {
141	  return typeof configSchema === "function" ? configSchema() : configSchema;
142	}
143	
144	/**
145	 * Canonical entry helper for non-channel plugins.
146	 *
147	 * Use this for provider, tool, command, service, memory, and context-engine
148	 * plugins. Channel plugins should use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` from
149	 * `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` so they inherit the channel capability wiring.
150	 */
151	export function definePluginEntry({
152	  id,
153	  name,
154	  description,
155	  kind,
156	  configSchema = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
157	  register,
158	}: DefinePluginEntryOptions): DefinedPluginEntry {
159	  return {
160	    id,
161	    name,
162	    description,
163	    ...(kind ? { kind } : {}),
164	    configSchema: resolvePluginConfigSchema(configSchema),
165	    register,
166	  };
167	}

I need to understand the implementation of an existing channel plugin in the OpenClaw repository so I can replicate the pattern for a new "PulseBoard" channel plugin.

Please find and read ALL files for an IRC channel plugin (or if IRC doesn't exist, use Nostr, Matrix, or Zalo — whichever is a simpler channel plugin). Read every file in the plugin package including:
- `package.json`
- `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest
- `tsconfig.json`
- All TypeScript source files under `src/`
- Any `api.ts` or `runtime-api.ts`
- Any test files

Also find and read the `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or equivalent workspace config at the repo root to understand how plugin packages are registered.

Report ALL file contents in full so I can use them as a template.

content.content:
1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.

1	# Plugin SDK Boundary
2	
3	This directory is the public contract between plugins and core. Changes here
4	can affect bundled plugins and third-party plugins.
5	
6	## Source Of Truth
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
14	- Definition files:
15	  - `package.json`
16	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
17	  - `src/plugin-sdk/entrypoints.ts`
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
22	
23	## Boundary Rules
24	
25	- Prefer narrow, purpose-built subpaths over broad convenience re-exports.
26	- Do not expose implementation convenience from `src/channels/**`,
27	  `src/agents/**`, `src/plugins/**`, or other internals unless you are
28	  intentionally promoting a supported public contract.
29	- Prefer `api.runtime` or a focused SDK facade over telling extensions to reach
30	  into host internals directly.
31	- When core or tests need bundled plugin helpers, expose them through
32	  the plugin package `api.ts` and a matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade
33	  instead of importing plugin-private `src/**` files or `onboard.js`
34	  directly.
35	
36	## Expanding The Boundary
37	
38	- Additive, backwards-compatible changes are the default.
39	- When adding or changing a public subpath, keep these aligned:
40	  - docs in `docs/plugins/*`
41	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
42	  - `src/plugin-sdk/entrypoints.ts`
43	  - `package.json` exports
44	  - API baseline and export checks
45	- If the seam is for bundled-provider onboarding/config helpers, update the
46	  generated plugin facades instead of teaching core tests or commands to reach
47	  into private extension files.
48	- Breaking removals or renames are major-version work, not drive-by cleanup.
49

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	# Channels Boundary
2	
3	`src/channels/**` is core channel implementation. Plugin authors should not
4	import from this tree directly.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	- Definition files:
13	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`
14	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`
15	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`
16	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
17	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
18	
19	## Boundary Rules
20	
21	- Keep extension-facing channel surfaces flowing through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`
22	  instead of direct imports from `src/channels/**`.
23	- When a bundled or third-party channel needs a new seam, add a typed SDK
24	  contract or facade first.
25	- Remember that shared channel changes affect both built-in and extension
26	  channels. Check routing, pairing, allowlists, command gating, onboarding, and
27	  reply behavior across the full set.
28

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

## Plugin SDK Contracts for Channel Plugins - Complete Report

Based on my exploration of the OpenClaw repository, here's a comprehensive guide to the channel plugin SDK contracts:

### File Locations and Contents

#### 1. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`**

This file is the re-export facade for channel plugin types. It exports:
- All core channel contract types from `src/channels/plugins/types.js`
- Serves as the single public entry point for channel types

#### 2. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`**

Contains the main channel plugin entry point helpers and re-exports:

**Key Exports:**
- `defineChannelPluginEntry<TPlugin>()` - The canonical helper for channel plugin entries
  - Parameters: `id`, `name`, `description`, `plugin`, `configSchema`, `setRuntime`, `registerCliMetadata`, `registerFull`
  - Returns a plugin entry with proper channel capability wiring
  
- `defineSetupPluginEntry<TPlugin>()` - Minimal setup-entry helper for separate setup files

- `createChatChannelPlugin<TResolvedAccount>()` - Higher-level builder for chat-style channels
  - Composes: scoped DM security, text pairing, reply threading, attached send results
  - Parameters: `base` (plugin base), `security`, `pairing`, `threading`, `outbound`

- `createChannelPluginBase<TResolvedAccount>()` - Shared base for channels overriding optional surfaces
  - Includes helpers for: `setupWizard`, `capabilities`, `agentPrompt`, `streaming`, `reload`, `gatewayMethods`, `configSchema`, `config`, `security`, `groups`

- `buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute()` - Builds canonical outbound session route payload
  - Parameters: `cfg`, `agentId`, `channel`, `accountId`, `peer` (kind/id), `chatType`, `from`, `to`, `threadId`

- Helper functions: `stripChannelTargetPrefix()`, `stripTargetKindPrefix()`

- Re-exports from plugin-entry.ts and other SDK modules

#### 3. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`**

Base plugin entry definitions:

**Key Exports:**
- `definePluginEntry()` - Canonical entry helper for non-channel plugins
  - Parameters: `id`, `name`, `description`, `kind`, `configSchema`, `register`
  - Used for providers, tools, commands, services, memory, context-engine plugins

#### 4. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`**

**The main channel plugin contract:**

```typescript
export type ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown, Audit = unknown> = {
  id: ChannelId;
  meta: ChannelMeta;
  capabilities: ChannelCapabilities;
  defaults?: {
    queue?: {
      debounceMs?: number;
    };
  };
  reload?: { configPrefixes: string[]; noopPrefixes?: string[] };
  setupWizard?: ChannelPluginSetupWizard;
  config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema;
  setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;
  pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter;
  security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter;
  mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter;
  outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter;
  status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>;
  gatewayMethods?: string[];
  gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter;
  approvalCapability?: ChannelApprovalCapability;
  elevated?: ChannelElevatedAdapter;
  commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter;
  lifecycle?: ChannelLifecycleAdapter;
  approvals?: ChannelApprovalAdapter;
  allowlist?: ChannelAllowlistAdapter;
  bindings?: ChannelConfiguredBindingProvider;
  conversationBindings?: ChannelConversationBindingSupport;
  streaming?: ChannelStreamingAdapter;
  threading?: ChannelThreadingAdapter;
  messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter;
  agentPrompt?: ChannelAgentPromptAdapter;
  directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter;
  resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter;
  actions?: ChannelMessageActionAdapter;
  heartbeat?: ChannelHeartbeatAdapter;
  agentTools?: ChannelAgentToolFactory | ChannelAgentTool[];
};
```

**Key Type Definitions:**

- `ChannelId` - Channel identifier (ChatChannelId or string)
- `ChannelMeta` - User-facing metadata for docs, pickers, setup surfaces
- `ChannelCapabilities` - Static capability flags (chatTypes, polls, reactions, edit, unsend, reply, effects, groupManagement, threads, media, nativeCommands, blockStreaming)
- `ChannelConfigSchema` - JSON-schema-like config with runtime validation
- `ChannelConfigUiHint` - UI hints for config fields (label, help, tags, advanced, sensitive, placeholder, itemTemplate)

#### 5. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`**

Core channel types and contracts (3000+ lines):

**Main Types:**
- `ChannelAgentTool` - Agent tool with optional `ownerOnly` flag
- `ChannelAgentToolFactory` - Lazy factory for tools dependent on config
- `ChannelCapabilities` - Chat types and feature support
- `ChannelMeta` - Display metadata (label, selectionLabel, docsPath, blurb, order, aliases, markdownCapable, etc.)
- `ChannelAccountSnapshot` - Runtime account state (enabled, configured, linked, running, connected, reconnectAttempts, etc.)
- `ChannelAccountState` - "linked" | "not linked" | "configured" | "not configured" | "enabled" | "disabled"
- `ChannelGroupContext` - Context for group operations
- `ChannelSetupInput` - Shared setup input bag
- `ChannelStatusIssue` - Status problem reporting (kind: intent|permissions|config|auth|runtime)

**Message Routing Types:**
- `ChannelOutboundSessionRoute` - Canonical route payload with sessionKey, baseSessionKey, peer, chatType, from, to, threadId
- `ChannelMessagingAdapter` - Message routing and payload shaping
  - `normalizeTarget(raw)` - Normalize target strings
  - `resolveSessionConversation({kind, rawId})` - Session conversation parsing
  - `resolveSessionTarget({kind, id, threadId})` - Get sendable target
  - `parseExplicitTarget({raw})` - Parse explicit targets
  - `inferTargetChatType({to})` - Infer chat type before directory lookup
  - `resolveOutboundSessionRoute()` - Build session route after target resolution
  - `targetResolver` - Custom fallback for native targets

**Threading Types:**
- `ChannelThreadingAdapter` - Thread support
  - `resolveReplyToMode()` - Off/first/all
  - `allowExplicitReplyTagsWhenOff` - Allow reply tags even when off
  - `buildToolContext()` - Build threading context for tools
  - `resolveAutoThreadId()` - Resolve implicit thread
  - `resolveReplyTransport()` - Resolve reply transport
  - `resolveFocusedBinding()` - Resolve focused conversation binding
- `ChannelThreadingContext` - Message thread context
- `ChannelThreadingToolContext` - Thread context for tools

**Message Action Types:**
- `ChannelMessageActionName` - Action names from discovery list
- `ChannelMessageActionDiscoveryContext` - Discovery inputs
- `ChannelMessageToolDiscovery` - Actions, capabilities, and schema for message tool
- `ChannelMessageActionContext` - Execution context for message tool actions
- `ChannelMessageActionAdapter` - Action surface
  - `describeMessageTool(params)` - Unified discovery
  - `supportsAction({action})` - Check action support
  - `requiresTrustedRequesterSender()` - Require trusted sender
  - `extractToolSend()` - Extract send target
  - `handleAction()` - Execute action

**Poll Types:**
- `ChannelPollContext` - Normalized poll context
- `ChannelPollResult` - Poll creation result

#### 6. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`**

Adapter contracts for channel capabilities:

**Setup & Config Adapters:**
- `ChannelSetupAdapter` - Account setup flow
  - `resolveAccountId()` - Resolve or create account ID
  - `resolveBindingAccountId()` - Get account for binding
  - `applyAccountName()` - Apply display name
  - `applyAccountConfig()` - Apply setup input to config
  - `afterAccountConfigWritten()` - Cleanup after config write
  - `validateInput()` - Validate setup input

- `ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Config resolution
  - `listAccountIds()` - Get all account IDs
  - `resolveAccount()` - Get typed account object
  - `isEnabled()`, `isConfigured()` - Check state
  - `describeAccount()` - Build account snapshot

**Message Sending:**
- `ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Outbound delivery
  - `deliveryMode` - "direct" | "gateway" | "hybrid"
  - `chunker()` - Text chunking function
  - `textChunkLimit` - Max characters per chunk
  - `normalizePayload()` - Transform reply payload
  - `shouldSuppressLocalPayloadPrompt()` - Skip local approval
  - `beforeDeliverPayload()` - Pre-delivery hook
  - `resolveTarget()` - Validate recipient
  - `sendText()`, `sendMedia()`, `sendPoll()` - Send methods
  - `sendPayload()`, `sendFormattedText()`, `sendFormattedMedia()` - Rich sending

**Security & Pairing:**
- `ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - DM security
  - `resolveDmPolicy()` - Get security policy
  - `collectWarnings()` - Collect security warnings

- `ChannelPairingAdapter` - Device/user pairing
  - `idLabel` - Human-readable ID label
  - `normalizeAllowEntry()` - Normalize allow-list entries
  - `notifyApproval()` - Notify after approval

**Group Operations:**
- `ChannelGroupAdapter` - Group context
  - `resolveRequireMention()` - Mention requirement
  - `resolveGroupIntroHint()` - Introduction hint
  - `resolveToolPolicy()` - Tool policy for group

**Gateway & Lifecycle:**
- `ChannelGatewayAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Account gateway lifecycle
  - `startAccount()` - Start account connection
  - `stopAccount()` - Stop connection
  - `loginWithQrStart()`, `loginWithQrWait()` - QR login flow
  - `logoutAccount()` - Logout
  - Includes `channelRuntime` field for SDK features (reply, routing, text, session, media, commands, groups, pairing)

- `ChannelLifecycleAdapter` - Config/account lifecycle hooks
  - `onAccountConfigChanged()` - React to config changes
  - `onAccountRemoved()` - React to account removal

**Status & Monitoring:**
- `ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` - Status queries
  - `probeAccount()` - Health check
  - `auditAccount()` - Full audit
  - `buildAccountSnapshot()` - Build status snapshot
  - `formatCapabilitiesProbe()` - Format probe for display
  - `buildCapabilitiesDiagnostics()` - Diagnostics display
  - `resolveAccountState()` - Current state
  - `collectStatusIssues()` - Detect problems

**Directory & Targeting:**
- `ChannelDirectoryAdapter` - User/group discovery
  - `self()` - Get self identity
  - `listPeers()`, `listPeersLive()` - User discovery
  - `listGroups()`, `listGroupsLive()` - Group discovery
  - `listGroupMembers()` - Get members

- `ChannelResolverAdapter` - Target resolution
  - `resolveTargets()` - Resolve user/group IDs

**Advanced:**
- `ChannelAuthAdapter` - Auth flow control
- `ChannelCommandAdapter` - Command enforcement
- `ChannelApprovalAdapter` - Approval delivery/rendering
- `ChannelAllowlistAdapter` - Allow-list management
- `ChannelConversationBindingSupport` - Conversation binding lifecycle
- `ChannelHeartbeatAdapter` - Readiness checks
- `ChannelMentionAdapter` - Mention stripping

#### 7. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/plugins/types.ts`**

(File too large - 27515 tokens - but provides general plugin infrastructure)

Key exports include provider, tool, command, service, and speech provider contracts.

#### 8. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775467635738-ada895/src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts`**

Provider contract registry and loader infrastructure.

#### 9. **AGENTS.md Files**

- **`src/plugin-sdk/AGENTS.md`** - Documents the plugin SDK boundary and public contracts
- **`src/channels/AGENTS.md`** - Documents channel boundary and public contracts

---

## Summary: What a Channel Plugin Must Implement

### 1. **Plugin Entry Point Structure**

```typescript
// In src/plugins/<id>.ts or similar
import { defineChannelPluginEntry, createChatChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "my-channel",
  name: "My Channel",
  description: "Integration with My Service",
  plugin: createChatChannelPlugin<MyResolvedAccount>({
    base: {
      id: "my-channel",
      meta: { /* ChannelMeta */ },
      setup: { /* ChannelSetupAdapter */ },
      config: { /* ChannelConfigAdapter<MyResolvedAccount> */ },
      capabilities: {
        chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
        media: true,
        threads: true,
      },
    },
    security: { /* ChannelSecurityAdapter<MyResolvedAccount> */ },
    pairing: { /* ChannelPairingAdapter */ },
    threading: { /* ChannelThreadingAdapter */ },
    outbound: { /* ChannelOutboundAdapter */ },
  }),
  configSchema: { /* OpenClawPluginConfigSchema */ },
});
```

### 2. **Required TypeScript Interfaces**

A channel plugin must implement:
1. **`ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount>`** - Main plugin interface with all adapters
2. **`ChannelMeta`** - User-facing metadata
3. **`ChannelCapabilities`** - Feature support flags
4. **`ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>`** - Config management
5. At least one of:
   - `ChannelSetupAdapter` - Account setup flow
   - `ChannelGatewayAdapter` - Connection lifecycle
   - `ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Sending messages

### 3. **Chat Types & Message Routing**

- `chatType` values: "direct" | "group" | "channel"
- Routes use `ChannelOutboundSessionRoute` with `sessionKey`, `peer` (kind/id), `from`, `to`, `threadId`
- Message routing through `ChannelMessagingAdapter` handles target normalization and session construction

### 4. **Configuration Schema Fields**

- Required manifest field: `configSchema` with `schema`, `uiHints`, optional `runtime` validator
- Configuration applied via `ChannelSetupAdapter.applyAccountConfig()`
- Account management through `ChannelConfigAdapter`

### 5. **Key Adapter Contracts**

Each adapter represents a capability:
- **Setup**: Initial account configuration
- **Config**: Runtime config resolution and account management
- **Outbound**: Message delivery (text, media, polls)
- **Security**: DM policy enforcement
- **Pairing**: Device/user authorization
- **Threading**: Reply-to and thread support
- **Gateway**: Long-running connection lifecycle
- **Status**: Health checks and diagnostics
- **Directory**: User/group discovery
- **Auth**: Login/logout flows

---

This comprehensive structure ensures:
- Type safety across plugin boundaries
- Consistent message routing and delivery
- Proper account/multi-instance management
- Security policy enforcement
- Integration with core agent systems

## Summary: What a Channel Plugin Must Implement

### 1. **Plugin Entry Point Structure**

```typescript
// In src/plugins/<id>.ts or similar
import { defineChannelPluginEntry, createChatChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "my-channel",
  name: "My Channel",
  description: "Integration with My Service",
  plugin: createChatChannelPlugin<MyResolvedAccount>({
    base: {
      id: "my-channel",
      meta: { /* ChannelMeta */ },
      setup: { /* ChannelSetupAdapter */ },
      config: { /* ChannelConfigAdapter<MyResolvedAccount> */ },
      capabilities: {
        chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
        media: true,
        threads: true,
      },
    },
    security: { /* ChannelSecurityAdapter<MyResolvedAccount> */ },
    pairing: { /* ChannelPairingAdapter */ },
    threading: { /* ChannelThreadingAdapter */ },
    outbound: { /* ChannelOutboundAdapter */ },
  }),
  configSchema: { /* OpenClawPluginConfigSchema */ },
});
```

### 2. **Required TypeScript Interfaces**

A channel plugin must implement:
1. **`ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount>`** - Main plugin interface with all adapters
2. **`ChannelMeta`** - User-facing metadata
3. **`ChannelCapabilities`** - Feature support flags
4. **`ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>`** - Config management
5. At least one of:
   - `ChannelSetupAdapter` - Account setup flow
   - `ChannelGatewayAdapter` - Connection lifecycle
   - `ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Sending messages

### 3. **Chat Types & Message Routing**

- `chatType` values: "direct" | "group" | "channel"
- Routes use `ChannelOutboundSessionRoute` with `sessionKey`, `peer` (kind/id), `from`, `to`, `threadId`
- Message routing through `ChannelMessagingAdapter` handles target normalization and session construction

### 4. **Configuration Schema Fields**

- Required manifest field: `configSchema` with `schema`, `uiHints`, optional `runtime` validator
- Configuration applied via `ChannelSetupAdapter.applyAccountConfig()`
- Account management through `ChannelConfigAdapter`

### 5. **Key Adapter Contracts**

Each adapter represents a capability:
- **Setup**: Initial account configuration
- **Config**: Runtime config resolution and account management
- **Outbound**: Message delivery (text, media, polls)
- **Security**: DM policy enforcement
- **Pairing**: Device/user authorization
- **Threading**: Reply-to and thread support
- **Gateway**: Long-running connection lifecycle
- **Status**: Health checks and diagnostics
- **Directory**: User/group discovery
- **Auth**: Login/logout flows

---

This comprehensive structure ensures:
- Type safety across plugin boundaries
- Consistent message routing and delivery
- Proper account/multi-instance management
- Security policy enforcement
- Integration with core agent systems

## Report: OpenClaw Bundled Workspace Plugin Structure

Based on my exploration of the OpenClaw repository, here is a comprehensive guide to understanding the bundled channel plugin architecture:

---

### **Root Directory Path of Bundled Plugin Workspace**

`/extensions` (at repository root)

This directory contains **94 bundled plugins** covering channels, providers, core services, and media handlers.

---

### **Boundary Documentation**

Both `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` (symlink) are present at `/extensions/AGENTS.md` and establish the plugin boundary rules. Key points:

- Plugins should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths only
- Metadata must be kept accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json`
- Plugin code should not reach into core internals or other plugin internals
- Treat `src/**` files as private unless exported through `api.ts`

### **Main Entry Points: `index.ts`**

**IRC pattern:**
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

**Telegram pattern:**
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { telegramPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
```

**Nostr pattern (with HTTP registration):**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { nostrPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { createNostrProfileHttpHandler } from "./src/nostr-profile-http.js";
import { getNostrRuntime, setNostrRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "nostr",
  name: "Nostr",
  description: "Nostr DM channel plugin via NIP-04",
  plugin: nostrPlugin,
  setRuntime: setNostrRuntime,
  registerFull(api) {
    // Custom HTTP route registration
    const httpHandler = createNostrProfileHttpHandler({ ... });
    api.registerHttpRoute({
      path: "/api/channels/nostr",
      auth: "gateway",
      match: "prefix",
      handler: httpHandler,
    });
  },
});
```

## **Key Patterns for Creating "PulseBoard" Channel Plugin**

Based on this analysis, here's what you need to replicate:

1. **Create the directory:** `/extensions/pulseboard/`

2. **Core files (required):**
   - `openclaw.plugin.json` - metadata
   - `package.json` - npm metadata with `openclaw` block
   - `index.ts` - main entry using `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
   - `setup-entry.ts` - setup using `defineSetupPluginEntry()`
   - `api.ts` - public exports (re-export from src/)
   - `channel-config-api.ts` - optional channel config hooks

3. **Source implementation (`src/`):**
   - `channel.ts` - core plugin using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
   - `runtime.ts` - runtime hooks via `setRuntime`
   - `accounts.ts` - account management
   - `send.ts` - outbound message handling
   - `inbound.ts` - inbound message routing
   - `normalize.ts` - target normalization
   - `probe.ts` - connectivity validation
   - `config-schema.ts` - configuration validation
   - `setup-surface.ts` - setup UI/wizard
   - `types.ts` - type definitions

4. **SDK imports:**
   - Always import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`
   - Use `createChatChannelPlugin()` for the base plugin
   - Use `defineChannelPluginEntry()` in index.ts

5. **Testing:** Include `.test.ts` files alongside implementation files

The IRC plugin is the leanest reference implementation (40 files). Telegram is feature-rich with 212 files showing advanced patterns (approvals, threading, inline buttons, etc.).

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report for the user.

## Report: OpenClaw Bundled Workspace Plugin Structure

Based on my exploration of the OpenClaw repository, here is a comprehensive guide to understanding the bundled channel plugin architecture:

---

### **Root Directory Path of Bundled Plugin Workspace**

`/extensions` (at repository root)

This directory contains **94 bundled plugins** covering channels, providers, core services, and media handlers.

---

### **Boundary Documentation**

Both `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` (symlink) are present at `/extensions/AGENTS.md` and establish the plugin boundary rules. Key points:

- Plugins should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths only
- Metadata must be kept accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json`
- Plugin code should not reach into core internals or other plugin internals
- Treat `src/**` files as private unless exported through `api.ts`

---

### **IRC Channel Plugin (Minimal Example)**

**Root:** `/extensions/irc`

**File Count:** 40 files

**Directory Structure:**

```
extensions/irc/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          (plugin manifest)
├── package.json                   (npm package metadata)
├── index.ts                       (main entry point)
├── setup-entry.ts                 (setup/configuration entry)
├── api.ts                         (public API exports)
├── channel-config-api.ts          (channel config hooks)
└── src/                           (implementation - private by default)
    ├── channel.ts                 (core plugin: createChatChannelPlugin)
    ├── runtime.ts                 (runtime hooks: setIrcRuntime)
    ├── runtime-api.ts
    ├── accounts.ts
    ├── send.ts                    (outbound message handling)
    ├── inbound.ts                 (inbound message handling)
    ├── normalize.ts               (target/peer normalization)
    ├── policy.ts                  (group/mention policies)
    ├── monitor.ts                 (connection monitoring)
    ├── probe.ts                   (connectivity check)
    ├── config-schema.ts
    ├── setup-core.ts
    ├── setup-surface.ts           (setup UI/wizard)
    ├── client.ts                  (IRC client implementation)
    ├── protocol.ts                (IRC protocol handling)
    ├── types.ts
    ├── connect-options.ts
    ├── control-chars.ts
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts
    └── [test files matching above with .test.ts suffix]
```

---

### **Telegram Channel Plugin (Complex Example)**

**Root:** `/extensions/telegram`

**File Count:** 212 files (much more complex with nested subdirectories)

**Key Top-Level Files:**

```
extensions/telegram/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          (plugin manifest)
├── package.json                   (with grammy dependencies)
├── index.ts                       (main entry point)
├── setup-entry.ts                 (setup entry)
├── api.ts                         (extensive public API exports)
├── allow-from.ts
├── channel-config-api.ts
├── runtime-api.ts
├── session-key-api.ts
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts                 (main plugin implementation)
    ├── channel.setup.ts
    ├── accounts.ts
    ├── runtime.ts
    ├── send.ts
    ├── outbound-adapter.ts
    ├── action-runtime.ts
    ├── action-threading.ts
    ├── approval-native.ts
    ├── api-fetch.ts
    ├── directory-config.ts
    ├── exec-approval-forwarding.ts
    ├── exec-approvals.ts
    ├── group-policy.ts
    ├── inline-buttons.ts
    ├── model-buttons.ts
    ├── normalize.ts
    ├── outbound-params.ts
    ├── probe.ts
    ├── reaction-level.ts
    ├── shared.ts
    ├── status-issues.ts
    ├── targets.ts
    ├── token.ts
    ├── thread-bindings.ts
    ├── threading-tool-context.ts
    ├── session-conversation.ts
    ├── sticker-cache.ts
    ├── update-offset-store.ts
    ├── button-types.ts
    ├── bot/
    │   ├── helpers.ts
    │   └── types.ts
    ├── test-support/
    └── [test files matching above]
```

---

### **Nostr Channel Plugin (Third Example)**

**Root:** `/extensions/nostr`

**File Count:** 38 files

**Directory Structure:**

```
extensions/nostr/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          (plugin manifest)
├── package.json                   (with nostr-tools dependencies)
├── README.md
├── CHANGELOG.md
├── index.ts                       (main entry point)
├── setup-entry.ts                 (setup entry)
├── api.ts                         (public API exports)
├── runtime-api.ts
├── setup-api.ts
├── test-api.ts                    (test utilities)
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts                 (core plugin)
    ├── runtime.ts
    ├── config-schema.ts
    ├── nostr-bus.ts               (Nostr protocol abstraction)
    ├── nostr-profile.ts           (profile management)
    ├── nostr-profile-http.ts      (HTTP profile endpoint)
    ├── nostr-profile-import.ts
    ├── nostr-state-store.ts       (state persistence)
    ├── setup-surface.ts           (setup UI)
    ├── session-route.ts
    ├── seen-tracker.ts
    ├── default-relays.ts
    ├── metrics.ts
    ├── types.ts
    ├── test-fixtures.ts
    └── [test files: inbound, outbound, fuzz, integration tests]
```

---

### **Plugin Manifest Files: `openclaw.plugin.json`**

**IRC manifest:**
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**Telegram manifest:**
```json
{
  "id": "telegram",
  "channels": ["telegram"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**Nostr manifest:**
```json
{
  "id": "nostr",
  "channels": ["nostr"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### **Package.json Configuration Pattern**

**IRC:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**Telegram:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/telegram",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@grammyjs/runner": "^2.0.3",
    "@grammyjs/transformer-throttler": "^1.2.1",
    "grammy": "^1.41.1"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "telegram",
      "label": "Telegram",
      "selectionLabel": "Telegram (Bot API)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/telegram",
      "markdownCapable": true
    },
    "bundle": {
      "stageRuntimeDependencies": true
    }
  }
}
```

**Nostr (includes npm publishing config):**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nostr",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "dependencies": {
    "nostr-tools": "^2.23.3"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "nostr",
      "label": "Nostr",
      "selectionLabel": "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/nostr",
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nostr",
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "release": {
      "publishToNpm": true
    }
  }
}
```

---

### **Main Entry Points: `index.ts`**

**IRC pattern:**
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

**Telegram pattern:**
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { telegramPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setTelegramRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { telegramPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setTelegramRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "telegram",
  name: "Telegram",
  description: "Telegram channel plugin",
  plugin: telegramPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setTelegramRuntime,
});
```

**Nostr pattern (with HTTP registration):**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { nostrPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { createNostrProfileHttpHandler } from "./src/nostr-profile-http.js";
import { getNostrRuntime, setNostrRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "nostr",
  name: "Nostr",
  description: "Nostr DM channel plugin via NIP-04",
  plugin: nostrPlugin,
  setRuntime: setNostrRuntime,
  registerFull(api) {
    // Custom HTTP route registration
    const httpHandler = createNostrProfileHttpHandler({ ... });
    api.registerHttpRoute({
      path: "/api/channels/nostr",
      auth: "gateway",
      match: "prefix",
      handler: httpHandler,
    });
  },
});
```

---

### **Setup Entry Points: `setup-entry.ts`**

All three plugins follow the same pattern:

```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { [channelName]Plugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry([channelName]Plugin);
```

---

### **Public API Files: `api.ts`**

**IRC (minimal, re-exports):**
```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

**Telegram (extensive, many sub-modules):**
```typescript
export * from "./src/account-inspect.js";
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/action-threading.js";
export * from "./src/allow-from.js";
export * from "./src/api-fetch.js";
export * from "./src/bot/helpers.js";
export * from "./src/directory-config.js";
export * from "./src/exec-approval-forwarding.js";
export * from "./src/exec-approvals.js";
export * from "./src/group-policy.js";
export * from "./src/inline-buttons.js";
export * from "./src/model-buttons.js";
export * from "./src/normalize.js";
export * from "./src/outbound-adapter.js";
export * from "./src/outbound-params.js";
export * from "./src/probe.js";
export * from "./src/reaction-level.js";
export * from "./src/sticker-cache.js";
export * from "./src/status-issues.js";
export * from "./src/targets.js";
export * from "./src/update-offset-store.js";
export type { TelegramButtonStyle, TelegramInlineButtons } from "./src/button-types.js";
export type { StickerMetadata } from "./src/bot/types.js";
export type { TelegramTokenResolution } from "./src/token.js";
```

---

## **Key Patterns for Creating "PulseBoard" Channel Plugin**

Based on this analysis, here's what you need to replicate:

1. **Create the directory:** `/extensions/pulseboard/`

2. **Core files (required):**
   - `openclaw.plugin.json` - metadata
   - `package.json` - npm metadata with `openclaw` block
   - `index.ts` - main entry using `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
   - `setup-entry.ts` - setup using `defineSetupPluginEntry()`
   - `api.ts` - public exports (re-export from src/)
   - `channel-config-api.ts` - optional channel config hooks

3. **Source implementation (`src/`):**
   - `channel.ts` - core plugin using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
   - `runtime.ts` - runtime hooks via `setRuntime`
   - `accounts.ts` - account management
   - `send.ts` - outbound message handling
   - `inbound.ts` - inbound message routing
   - `normalize.ts` - target normalization
   - `probe.ts` - connectivity validation
   - `config-schema.ts` - configuration validation
   - `setup-surface.ts` - setup UI/wizard
   - `types.ts` - type definitions

4. **SDK imports:**
   - Always import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`
   - Use `createChatChannelPlugin()` for the base plugin
   - Use `defineChannelPluginEntry()` in index.ts

5. **Testing:** Include `.test.ts` files alongside implementation files

The IRC plugin is the leanest reference implementation (40 files). Telegram is feature-rich with 212 files showing advanced patterns (approvals, threading, inline buttons, etc.).

The protocol supports a 4000-character chunk limit, with boards formatted as #board-name and DMs as @user-name, and I'm planning to handle delivery directly rather than through a gateway.

For the implementation, I need to decide on chat types (direct and group), set up authentication via API token from environment variables, create a setup wizard that prompts for server URL and username, and keep it lightweight without external dependencies since this is a fictional protocol. Now I'm looking at what the runtime API should export — examining how the IRC plugin structures its imports and what base functionality I'll need to build out for PulseBoard's own SDK layer. I'm seeing that the plugin SDK uses path aliases to map `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` to a dedicated file that re-exports types, but PulseBoard doesn't have this facade yet—I need to figure out how to structure the imports so extensions can still import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` following the same pattern. the IRC plugin. For PulseBoard, we won't have `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` yet, so I need to import directly from the published SDK subpaths instead. Let me trace where the IRC-specific imports actually come from — things like `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary`, `createAccountStatusSink`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, and `getChatChannelMeta` are re-exported through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`, but I should check if they're available through the core SDK paths like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared`, or `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` instead. I'm seeing that I need to track down where these utilities actually live in the SDK so I can set up the correct import paths for the runtime API wrapper.

The key realization is that `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` is just a facade that re-exports things from the core SDK, and since I can't create a new `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts` file due to the constraint of only adding files to the pulseboard extension directory, I'll need to import directly from whatever SDK subpaths are already available rather than going through a plugin-specific facade.

// Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

  BaseProbeResult,

  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

The IRC `runtime-api.ts` imports exclusively from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`. This is a dedicated facade at `src/plugin-sdk/irc.ts`. Since we can't modify existing files, we can't create `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts`.

But wait - the constraint says "Do NOT modify any existing files. Only create new files in the pulseboard extension directory." So we can only create files under `extensions/pulseboard/`. We can't create `src/plugin-sdk/pulseboard.ts`.

This means our `runtime-api.ts` needs to import directly from the available SDK subpaths.

- `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` → `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`

I need to trace through which of these are actually exported from the `irc` subpath and which ones might need alternative import paths or aren't available at all.

Looking at the IRC channel imports, I can see it's already using several valid subpaths like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers`, and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`. These are all legitimate entry points I can work with. Now I need to figure out what's coming from `runtime-api.js` and whether those exports are accessible through the public SDK paths. I need to verify which SDK subpaths actually export the functions I'm importing—specifically checking if `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary` is available from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` or if it comes from a different module like the irc-specific exports. I'm weighing two approaches: either have each file import directly from the public SDK subpaths, or create a local `runtime-api.ts` that acts as a barrel by importing from those same SDK paths rather than from a non-existent `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard`. The second option feels cleaner and more maintainable, so I'll go with that and check what `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` exposes to build out the barrel properly.

Now I'm ready to sketch out the full implementation plan for the PulseBoard plugin. The `runtime-api.ts` will re-export from various SDK subpaths like `core`, `status-helpers`, `account-id`, `channel-pairing`, and `channel-policy` rather than trying to import from a path that doesn't exist. For the plan document itself, I don't need to enumerate every single import—just describe at a high level what each file does and where it pulls from.

The plugin will live in `extensions/pulseboard/` and connect to a fictional PulseBoard protocol at `https://pulseboard.example.com` using bearer token authentication, with the bot operating under a configured username. I'm laying out the remaining source files that handle the core functionality—type definitions, configuration schemas with UI hints, the runtime store and internal SDK, account resolution, target normalization for routing messages, health checks, and both outbound and inbound message handling.

Then there's the connection lifecycle monitoring, setup adapters with config helpers, the setup wizard interface, group and mention policies, the PulseBoard API client wrapper, and finally the main plugin definition that ties everything together. I'm also including test coverage for accounts, normalization, and the channel module to ensure the critical paths work correctly.

Now I'm writing up the full plan document.

Looking at the key design decisions: I'm setting the text chunk limit to 4000 characters, supporting direct and group chat types with direct delivery mode, using `#board-name` for board addressing and `@username` for DMs, API token authentication, and requiring a server URL in config. The implementation stays pure TypeScript with `fetch` for HTTP calls and no external dependencies.

For the client interface, I'm defining a `PulseBoardClient` with methods to post messages, subscribe to incoming messages, disconnect, and check readiness status. The connection function takes options and returns a ready client instance, using `fetch` to send messages and handling subscriptions.

Now I'm setting up the monitor to poll the PulseBoard server for new messages and route them through `handlePulseBoardInbound` for processing.

input.content:
# Plan: Add PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

The user wants to add a fictional "PulseBoard" real-time bulletin board messaging
channel plugin to OpenClaw. PulseBoard supports named "boards" (group chat,
`#board-name` prefix) and direct messages (DM, `@username` prefix), with a
4000-character text chunk limit. The plugin must follow the same extension
structure as the existing IRC channel plugin (the simplest reference
implementation at `extensions/irc/`).

**Constraint:** Only create new files inside `extensions/pulseboard/`. Do not
modify any existing files.

---

## Reference Implementation

The IRC plugin (`extensions/irc/`) is the cleanest analog (40 files, no
external deps). All patterns below are derived from it.

**SDK entry points used** (all public, listed in
`scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`):
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` — `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`,
  `defineSetupPluginEntry`, `getChatChannelMeta`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`,
  `parseOptionalDelimitedEntries`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` — `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` — `describeAccountSnapshot`,
  `createAccountListHelpers`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution` — `resolveMergedAccountConfig`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from` — `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` — `adaptScopedAccountAccessor`,
  `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` — `buildChannelConfigSchema`,
  `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema`,
  `ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`, `requireOpenAllowFrom`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-lifecycle` — `createAccountStatusSink`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing` — `createChannelPairingController`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` — `composeAccountWarningCollectors`,
  `createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime` — `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`,
  `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared` — `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`,
  `resolveLoggerBackedRuntime`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-chunking` — `chunkTextForOutbound`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-payload` — `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`,
  type `OutboundReplyPayload`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-group-policy` — `GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL`,
  `resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy`, `resolveDefaultGroupPolicy`,
  `warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store` — `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime` — `readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy`,
  `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` — `createAllowFromSection`,
  `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`,
  `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`, `patchScopedAccountConfig`,
  `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `formatDocsLink`, type `ChannelSetupWizard`,
  type `WizardPrompter`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` — `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`,
  `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`, `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input` — `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime` — `convertMarkdownTables`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` — `resolveMarkdownTableMode`,
  types `DmPolicy`, `GroupPolicy`, `DmConfig`, `MarkdownConfig`,
  `GroupToolPolicyConfig`, `GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig`,
  `BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-contract` — type `BaseProbeResult`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-env` — type `RuntimeEnv`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod` — `z`

---

## Files to Create

All files live under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### Top-level files (7)

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`

Plugin manifest required for discovery:

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/package.json`

npm package with `openclaw` metadata block. No external deps needed (fictional
protocol). Key fields:

- `"name": "@openclaw/pulseboard"`
- `"version": "2026.4.1-beta.1"`
- `"type": "module"`
- `openclaw.extensions`: `["./index.ts"]`
- `openclaw.setupEntry`: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- `openclaw.install.minHostVersion`: `">=2026.4.1"`
- `openclaw.channel.id`: `"pulseboard"`
- `openclaw.channel.label`: `"PulseBoard"`
- `openclaw.channel.selectionLabel`: `"PulseBoard (Board + DM)"`
- `openclaw.channel.detailLabel`: `"PulseBoard"`
- `openclaw.channel.docsPath`: `"/channels/pulseboard"`
- `openclaw.channel.docsLabel`: `"pulseboard"`
- `openclaw.channel.blurb`: `"real-time bulletin board messaging with named boards and direct messages."`
- `openclaw.channel.aliases`: `["pulse-board", "pulse"]`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts`

Main entry point. Mirrors `extensions/irc/index.ts` exactly in pattern:

```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard real-time bulletin board channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseBoardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime,
});
```

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts`

Separate entry for setup surface (loaded only during onboarding):

```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin);
```

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts`

Public API barrel — re-exports for use by other packages or core tests:

```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/channel-config-api.ts`

Exposes the config schema for tooling and channel config surfaces:

```typescript
export { PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema } from "./src/config-schema.js";
```

---

### Source files under `src/` (11)

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/types.ts`

All TypeScript types for PulseBoard. Key types:

**`PulseBoardBoardConfig`** — per-board settings (mirrors `IrcChannelConfig`):
- `requireMention?: boolean`
- `tools?: GroupToolPolicyConfig`
- `toolsBySender?: Record<string, GroupToolPolicyConfig>`
- `skills?: string[]`
- `enabled?: boolean`
- `allowFrom?: Array<string | number>`
- `systemPrompt?: string`

**`PulseBoardAccountConfig`** — account-level config:
- `name?: string`
- `enabled?: boolean`
- `serverUrl?: string` — PulseBoard server base URL
- `token?: string` — API token
- `tokenFile?: string` — path to file containing token
- `username?: string` — bot username on PulseBoard
- `boards?: string[]` — boards to subscribe to on connect
- `dmPolicy?: DmPolicy` — default `"pairing"`
- `allowFrom?: Array<string | number>`
- `groupPolicy?: GroupPolicy` — default `"allowlist"`
- `groupAllowFrom?: Array<string | number>`
- `groups?: Record<string, PulseBoardBoardConfig>`
- `defaultTo?: string`
- `textChunkLimit?: number`
- `blockStreaming?: boolean`

**`PulseBoardConfig`** — root config (extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig`):
- `accounts?: Record<string, PulseBoardAccountConfig>`
- `defaultAccount?: string`

**`CoreConfig`** — augments `OpenClawConfig` with `channels.pulseboard`:
```typescript
type CoreConfig = OpenClawConfig & {
  channels?: OpenClawConfig["channels"] & {
    pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig;
  };
};
```

**`PulseBoardInboundMessage`**:
- `messageId: string`
- `target: string` — `#board-name` for groups, sender `@username` for DMs
- `senderId: string` — `@alice`
- `senderName?: string` — display name
- `text: string`
- `timestamp: number`
- `isGroup: boolean` — `true` = board post, `false` = DM

**`PulseBoardProbe`** — extends `BaseProbeResult<string>`:
- `serverUrl: string`
- `username: string`
- `latencyMs?: number`

Imports: types from `./runtime-api.js`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime-api.ts`

Private runtime barrel — consolidates imports from SDK paths so other
`src/` files import from one local barrel. Since there is no
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` facade, import directly from the
available public SDK subpaths:

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the bundled PulseBoard extension.

  getChatChannelMeta,

  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-group-policy";

  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,

} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime";

export type {
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
```

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts`

Runtime store — typed singleton for the PulseBoard gateway runtime.
Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts`:

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseBoardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");

export { getPulseBoardRuntime, setPulseBoardRuntime };
```

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/accounts.ts`

Account config resolution. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`.

**Exports:**
- `type ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` — resolved account shape:
  - `accountId: string`
  - `enabled: boolean`
  - `name?: string`
  - `configured: boolean` — `true` when `serverUrl`, `token`, and `username` are present
  - `serverUrl: string`
  - `token: string`
  - `tokenSource: "env" | "tokenFile" | "config" | "none"`
  - `username: string`
  - `config: PulseBoardAccountConfig`

- `listPulseBoardAccountIds(cfg)` — via `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", ...)`
- `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId(cfg)` — via `createAccountListHelpers`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })` — merges base + account config,
  resolves token from env `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `tokenFile`, or config field,
  resolves `serverUrl` from env `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL` or config,
  resolves `username` from env `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME` or config
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg)` — filters to enabled accounts

**Key implementation logic:**
- `mergedPulseBoardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId)` uses `resolveMergedAccountConfig`
  with `channelConfig: cfg.channels?.pulseboard`, `accounts: cfg.channels?.pulseboard?.accounts`
- `resolveToken(accountId, merged)` checks env `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN` (default account only),
  then `tokenFile`, then config `token`
- `configured` = `Boolean(serverUrl && token && username)`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`

Target/ID normalization for PulseBoard addressing. Key rules:
- Board targets start with `#` (e.g., `#general`, `#engineering`)
- DM targets start with `@` (e.g., `@alice`, `@bob`)
- Accept `pulseboard:` prefix and strip it

**Exports:**
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — `target.startsWith("#")`
- `isDmTarget(target: string): boolean` — `target.startsWith("@")`
- `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget(raw: string): string | undefined`
  — strips `pulseboard:` prefix, strips `board:` prefix (adds `#`),
  strips `user:` prefix (adds `@`), validates non-empty
- `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(raw: string): boolean`
  — `true` for valid `#` or `@`-prefixed non-whitespace strings
- `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(raw: string): string`
  — lowercases, strips `pulseboard:` and `user:` prefixes
- `normalizePulseBoardAllowlist(entries?: Array<string | number>): string[]`
- `buildPulseBoardAllowlistCandidates(message: PulseBoardInboundMessage): string[]`
  — returns `[senderId, senderName]` lowercased candidates
- `resolvePulseBoardAllowlistMatch(params): { allowed: boolean; source?: string }`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts`

Zod schema for PulseBoard config. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`.

**Schema structure:**
```
PulseBoardBoardSchema (group-level)
PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase (shared account fields)
PulseBoardAccountSchema (with superRefine for open dmPolicy validation)
PulseBoardConfigSchema (extends base, adds accounts + defaultAccount)
PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema (= buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseBoardConfigSchema, { uiHints }))
```

**Notable fields in `PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase`:**
- `serverUrl: z.string().optional()`
- `token: z.string().optional()`
- `tokenFile: z.string().optional()`
- `username: z.string().optional()`
- `boards: z.array(z.string()).optional()`
- `dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing")`
- `groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist")`
- `textChunkLimit: z.number().int().min(1).max(4000).optional()`
- Plus `allowFrom`, `groupAllowFrom`, `groups`, `defaultTo`, `blockStreaming`,
  `markdown`, `...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`

Imports from:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod`
- `./config-ui-hints.js`

**Export:** `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-ui-hints.ts`

UI hint labels/help text for config fields. Mirrors
`extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts`.

**Export:** `pulseBoardConfigUiHints` — `Record<string, ChannelConfigUiHint>` with
entries for `""` (section root), `serverUrl`, `token`, `tokenFile`, `username`,
`boards`, `dmPolicy`, `groupPolicy`, `textChunkLimit`.

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/client.ts`

Fictional PulseBoard protocol client. Since PulseBoard is imaginary, this
module defines the interface and a stub implementation that would normally
connect over WebSocket or HTTP polling.

**Interface `PulseBoardClient`:**
```typescript
interface PulseBoardClient {
  readonly username: string;
  isReady(): boolean;
  sendDirectMessage(toUser: string, text: string): void;
  sendBoardMessage(board: string, text: string): void;
  disconnect(reason?: string): void;
}
```

**Function `connectPulseBoardClient(opts): Promise<PulseBoardClient>`:**
- `opts`: `{ serverUrl, token, username, boards?, connectTimeoutMs?, abortSignal?,
  onMessage?, onError?, onNotice? }`
- Simulates a connection by constructing a client object
- In a real implementation this would open a WebSocket to `serverUrl` and
  authenticate with `token`
- `onMessage` callback receives `{ kind: "board" | "dm", senderId, senderName?,
  target, text, timestamp }` for inbound messages

This file has no SDK imports — pure TypeScript with a fictional protocol stub.

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/probe.ts`

Health-check function. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/probe.ts`.

**Export `probePulseBoard(cfg, opts?): Promise<PulseBoardProbe>`:**
1. `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId: opts?.accountId })`
2. Return `{ ok: false, error: "missing serverUrl, token, or username" }` if
   not configured
3. Attempt `connectPulseBoardClient(...)` with `connectTimeoutMs` timeout
4. On success: `disconnect("probe")`, return `{ ok: true, latencyMs, serverUrl, username }`
5. On error: return `{ ok: false, error: formatError(err), serverUrl, username }`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/send.ts`

Outbound message delivery. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/send.ts`.

**Types:**
- `type SendPulseBoardOptions = { cfg?, accountId?, replyTo?, target?, client? }`
- `type SendPulseBoardResult = { messageId: string; target: string }`

**Export `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts?): Promise<SendPulseBoardResult>`:**
1. Load config from runtime or opts
2. `resolvePulseBoardAccount(...)` — throw if not configured
3. Resolve target via `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget`
4. Convert markdown tables via `resolveMarkdownTableMode` + `convertMarkdownTables`
5. Append `[reply:${replyTo}]` suffix if `opts.replyTo` is set
6. If `opts.client?.isReady()`: use existing client
7. Otherwise: create transient `connectPulseBoardClient(...)`, send, disconnect
8. Pick `sendDirectMessage` vs `sendBoardMessage` based on `isDmTarget(target)`
9. Record outbound activity via `getPulseBoardRuntime().channel.activity.record(...)`
10. Return `{ messageId: makePulseBoardMessageId(), target }`

Helper `makePulseBoardMessageId()` — returns `pulseboard-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/inbound.ts`

Inbound message routing and allowlist enforcement. Mirrors
`extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts` (simplified).

**Export `handlePulseBoardInbound(params): Promise<void>`:**
Params: `{ message: PulseBoardInboundMessage, account: ResolvedPulseBoardAccount,
config: CoreConfig, runtime: RuntimeEnv, sendReply, statusSink? }`

Logic (mirrors IRC):
1. Resolve effective allow-from lists via `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists`
2. DM path: check `resolvePulseBoardAllowlistMatch`, enforce dm policy, gate pairing
   via `createChannelPairingController`, dispatch via `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`
3. Group/board path: check group policy, check group sender allowed, enforce
   `requireMention`, dispatch via `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`

Key helpers used from `./runtime-api.js`:
- `GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL`, `resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy`,
  `resolveDefaultGroupPolicy`, `readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy`,
  `resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists`, `createChannelPairingController`,
  `deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments`, `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase` (or equivalent)

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/monitor.ts`

Connection lifecycle and inbound event loop. Mirrors
`extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts`.

**Export `monitorPulseBoardProvider(opts): Promise<{ stop() }>`:**
Params: `{ accountId?, config?, runtime?, abortSignal?, statusSink?, onMessage? }`

Logic:
1. Load config, `resolvePulseBoardAccount`
2. Throw if not configured
3. `resolveLoggerBackedRuntime(...)` for logger
4. `connectPulseBoardClient(...)` with:
   - `boards: account.config.boards`
   - `abortSignal`
   - `onMessage` callback: build `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, record activity,
     call `handlePulseBoardInbound(...)` or custom `opts.onMessage`
   - `onError`, `onNotice` for logging
5. Log connected message
6. Return `{ stop: () => { client.disconnect("shutdown") } }`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-core.ts`

Config patch helpers used by the setup wizard. Mirrors
`extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts`.

**Exports:**
- `type PulseBoardSetupInput` (extends `ChannelSetupInput`) with extra fields:
  `serverUrl?, token?, username?, boards?`
- `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId, patch): CoreConfig`
  — via `patchScopedAccountConfig`
- `setPulseBoardAllowFrom(cfg, accountId, allowFrom): CoreConfig`
  — via `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`
- `setPulseBoardDmPolicy(cfg, accountId, dmPolicy): CoreConfig`
  — via `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`
- `setPulseBoardBoards(cfg, accountId, boards): CoreConfig`
  — patches `channels.pulseboard.boards`
- `pulseBoardSetupAdapter` — `ChannelSetupAdapter` that implements:
  - `resolveAccountId` — `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`
  - `applyAccountConfig` — calls `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig` with
    `{ serverUrl, token, username, boards }`
  - `validateInput` — requires `serverUrl`, `token`, `username`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts`

Interactive setup wizard. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`.

**Export `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`:**

Wizard steps (via `WizardPrompter`):
1. **Intro** — display `createStandardChannelSetupStatus` summary
2. **Server URL** — text prompt for `serverUrl`
3. **API Token** — sensitive text prompt for `token`; detect env `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`
4. **Username** — text prompt for `username`; detect env `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`
5. **Boards** — comma/newline list prompt for boards to join; validate `#` prefix
6. **Allow-from** — DM allowlist via `createAllowFromSection`
7. **DM policy** — select from `"pairing"` / `"open"` / `"closed"`
8. **Completion note** — show `formatDocsLink("/channels/pulseboard")`

---

#### `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts`

Core plugin definition. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`.

**Export `pulseBoardPlugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedPulseBoardAccount, PulseBoardProbe>`:**

```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "pulseboard",
    meta: { ...getChatChannelMeta("pulseboard"), quickstartAllowFrom: true },
    setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
    setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
    capabilities: {
      chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
      media: true,
      blockStreaming: true,
    },
    reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
    configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
    config: {
      ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter,     // createScopedChannelConfigAdapter
      isConfigured: (account) => account.configured,
      describeAccount: (account) => describeAccountSnapshot({ account, configured: account.configured,
        extra: { serverUrl: account.serverUrl, username: account.username, tokenSource: account.tokenSource } }),
    },
    groups: {
      resolveRequireMention: ({ cfg, accountId, groupId }) => { ... },
      resolveToolPolicy: ({ cfg, accountId, groupId }) => { ... },
    },
    messaging: {
      normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget,
      targetResolver: { looksLikeId: looksLikePulseBoardTargetId, hint: "<#board|@user>" },
    },
    resolver: {
      resolveTargets: async ({ inputs, kind }) => { /* normalize + validate */ },
    },
    directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({
      listPeers: async (params) => listPulseBoardDirectoryPeersFromConfig(params),
      listGroups: async (params) => { ... },
    }),
    status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter<ResolvedPulseBoardAccount, PulseBoardProbe>({
      defaultRuntime: createDefaultChannelRuntimeState(DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID),
      buildChannelSummary: ({ account, snapshot }) => ({ ...buildBaseChannelStatusSummary(snapshot),
        serverUrl: account.serverUrl, username: account.username, probe: snapshot.probe }),
      probeAccount: async ({ cfg, account, timeoutMs }) => probePulseBoard(cfg, { accountId: account.accountId, timeoutMs }),
      resolveAccountSnapshot: ({ account }) => ({ accountId: account.accountId, name: account.name,
        enabled: account.enabled, configured: account.configured,
        extra: { serverUrl: account.serverUrl, username: account.username } }),
    }),
    gateway: {
      startAccount: async (ctx) => {
        const statusSink = createAccountStatusSink({ accountId: ctx.accountId, setStatus: ctx.setStatus });
        if (!ctx.account.configured) throw new Error(`PulseBoard not configured for "${ctx.accountId}" ...`);
        await runStoppablePassiveMonitor({
          abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
          start: async () => monitorPulseBoardProvider({ accountId: ctx.account.accountId,
            config: ctx.cfg, runtime: ctx.runtime, abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal, statusSink }),
        });
      },
    },
  },
  pairing: {
    text: {
      idLabel: "pulseBoardUser",
      message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
      normalizeAllowEntry: (entry) => normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(entry),
      notify: async ({ id, message }) => {
        const target = normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(id);
        if (!target) throw new Error(`invalid PulseBoard pairing id: ${id}`);
        await sendMessagePulseBoard(`@${target}`, message);
      },
    },
  },
  security: {
    resolveDmPolicy: resolveIrcDmPolicy,  // via createScopedDmSecurityResolver
    collectWarnings: collectPulseBoardSecurityWarnings,
  },
  outbound: {
    base: {
      deliveryMode: "direct",
      chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
      chunkerMode: "markdown",
      textChunkLimit: 4000,  // PulseBoard's 4000-char limit
    },
    attachedResults: {
      channel: "pulseboard",
      sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) =>
        sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId }),
      sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) =>
        sendMessagePulseBoard(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text,
          { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId }),
    },
  },
});
```

---

### `pnpm-workspace.yaml` note

No modification needed — the workspace already uses `extensions/*` glob, so
`extensions/pulseboard` is automatically included.

---

## Critical Files to Read Before Implementing

1. `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` — master pattern for `createChatChannelPlugin`
2. `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts` — account resolution + `createAccountListHelpers`
3. `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts` — full inbound allowlist/pairing logic
4. `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts` — full wizard pattern
5. `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts` — `patchScopedAccountConfig`, setup adapter
6. `src/plugin-sdk/status-helpers.ts` — `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter` signature
7. `src/plugin-sdk/channel-lifecycle.ts` (or `.core.ts`) — `createAccountStatusSink`
8. `src/plugin-sdk/reply-chunking.ts` — confirm `chunkTextForOutbound` export

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript** — run `pnpm tsgo` (or `pnpm build`) from repo root; the new
   `extensions/pulseboard/**/*.ts` files must type-check cleanly.
2. **Lint** — run `pnpm check` from repo root; no lint errors in new files.
3. **Manifest discovery** — `openclaw.plugin.json` must parse and the `id` field
   must match `package.json → openclaw.channel.id` and the `index.ts`
   `defineChannelPluginEntry` id (`"pulseboard"` throughout).
4. **Unit test** (optional but encouraged) — add
   `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.test.ts` testing
   `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget` for board (`#foo`), DM (`@alice`),
   prefix stripping (`pulseboard:#foo`), and invalid inputs.
5. **Invariant test** — the repo's plugin invariant test at
   `src/plugins/contracts/` checks that `openclaw.install.npmSpec` equals
   package name and channel id alignment; verify nothing new fails via
   `pnpm test -- src/plugins`.
